steps: Map costs by class order: 1st class = $0 (free); 2nd class = $22.80 / 2 = $11.40 (half off); classes 3 through x = $22.80 each (regular price).
For x total classes (x \u2265 2), the number of regularly-priced classes is x \u2212 2.

steps.title: Graph the cost checkpoints
steps.content: In the Desmos rows, $x_1$ lists sample total class counts $2$, $3$, and $4$, while $y_1$ lists the matching costs. The model row $y=22.80(x-2)+11.40$ should pass through all three plotted points.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — 'f(x) = 22.80(x \u2212 2) + 11.40': Correctly multiplies regular price by (x \u2212 2) remaining classes and adds the half-price second class. Verified with x = 3 gives $34.20. ✓
  • Choice B — 'f(x) = 22.80x + 11.40': Charges regular price for all x classes plus the half-price cost, ignoring both the free first class and the discount on the second. Overcounts by $22.80 + $11.40 = $34.20. ✗
  • Choice C — 'f(x) = 22.80(x \u2212 1) + 11.40': Subtracts only 1 from x, as if only the free class is excluded, but the second class should also be excluded from the regular rate. For x = 3: 22.80(2) + 11.40 = $57.00, not $34.20. ✗
  • Choice D — 'f(x) = 22.80(x \u2212 1) + 11.40(x \u2212 2)': Applies the half-price rate to (x \u2212 2) classes instead of just one, which is incorrect; only the second class is half off. For x = 3: 22.80(2) + 11.40(1) = $57.00. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
